source.title: Tesoro Martinez Refinery Process Safety Culture Case Study - February 12, 2014 and March 10, 2014 Sulfuric Acid Release Incidents

event.top_event: Loss of Containment - Sulfuric Acid Release
actions_taken: Workers immediately went to nearby safety shower
Emergency responders transferred workers by helicopter and ambulance to UC Davis Regional Burn Center
Cal/OSHA required process shutdown from February 18, 2014 until February 28, 2014
Tesoro upgraded PPE requirements to include full acid suit and air-purifying respirator
Tesoro replaced failed tubing connector and adjacent inadequately tightened compression fitting
Tesoro commissioned Anamet, Inc. to conduct materials testing on failed tubing
event.summary: On February 12, 2014, two Tesoro Martinez refinery employees suffered first and second degree chemical burns while working to return a sulfuric acid sampling system to service following nonroutine maintenance. Concentrated sulfuric acid sprayed the workers following a mechanical integrity failure of a ¾-inch tubing connector adjacent to the acid sampling station. The tubing came apart at the connector when operators opened a valve to return the acid sampling system to service. Acid continued to spray from a nearly 100,000-gallon acid settler for over two hours, resulting in the release of approximately 84,000 pounds of sulfuric acid. Both workers were transported to a burn center and neither returned to work for over 150 days. The incident was classified as a Tier 1 process safety event (loss of primary containment). The CSB investigation found that weak process safety culture at the refinery, including inadequate mechanical integrity practices, insufficient PPE, poor sampling system design, and failure to learn from previous incidents, were causal to the incident.
recommendations: Implement inherently safer sulfuric acid sampling system design with closed-loop systems
Establish robust mechanical integrity program with mandatory leak-testing of tubing and fittings following repairs
Provide adequate and functional PPE for all workers exposed to sulfuric acid hazards
Conduct comprehensive process hazard analysis specifically evaluating sulfuric acid sampling system hazards
Strengthen process safety culture through management commitment and effective communication of safety lessons
Implement leading and lagging process safety indicators to identify trends and trigger preventive inspections
Enhance regulatory oversight through proactive and preventive inspections by state regulators
Establish effective management of change procedures for all equipment modifications
Implement corrective actions from previous safety culture assessments
Ensure effective incorporation and communication of lessons learned from previous incidents
key_phrases: failure to learn from previous incidents

hazards.name: Sulfuric Acid - Corrosive and Reactive Chemical
Flammable Hydrocarbon Layer in Acid Settler

threats.name: Mechanical Integrity Failure of Tubing Connector
Improper Maintenance and Repair of Sampling System
Weak Process Safety Culture and Management Commitment

consequences.name: Loss of Primary Containment - 84,000 pounds of Sulfuric Acid Released
Worker Incapacity - Extended Time Away from Work (150+ days)
Potential Escalation to Fire or Explosion from Flammable Hydrocarbon Layer

controls.name: Mechanical Integrity Program for Tubing and Fittings
controls.barrier_role: Prevent tubing connector failure through proper installation and maintenance
controls.lod_basis: Primary engineering control to prevent loss of containment

supporting_text: The report concluded that the sulfuric acid release on February 12, 2014 resulted from the failure of a ¾-inch diameter stainless steel tubing connector that came apart due to insufficient tightening between a tube and a compression joint
The Tesoro Martinez refinery did not require workers to leak-test tubing of this type following repairs
Following the February 12, 2014 incident, maintenance workers also found an inadequately tightened compression fitting for an adjacent sample station

controls.name: Leak Testing Procedure for Repaired Tubing and Fittings
controls.barrier_role: Detect mechanical integrity failures before system pressurization
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to verify integrity of repairs before returning system to service

supporting_text: Due to maintenance program deficiencies including gaps in planning, documentation, and communication, Tesoro did not effectively repair or leak-test the tubing prior to placing it back in service
The Tesoro Martinez refinery did not require workers to leak-test tubing of this type following repairs

controls.name: Inherently Safer Sampling System Design
controls.barrier_role: Eliminate or minimize worker exposure to sulfuric acid through superior engineering design
controls.lod_basis: Primary control using hierarchy of controls to eliminate hazard at source

supporting_text: In January 2010, the Tesoro Martinez refinery made a substantial financial investment in what the refinery asserted was a more robust, 'inherently safer' sampling system, which the refinery then failed to make operable and took out of service
On February 12, 2014, workers were putting the older, poorly designed system back into service when the incident occurred

Tesoro approved a project to install new closed loop sample systems in June 2009. Tesoro designed these systems to be a significant upgrade to the existing sample stations, with the goal of minimizing operator exposure to the sulfuric acid when sampling

controls.name: Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) for Acid Sampling
controls.barrier_role: Protect workers from sulfuric acid exposure during sampling operations
controls.lod_basis: Last line of defense to protect workers from chemical exposure

supporting_text: At the time of the incident, the two employees were wearing the personal protective equipment (PPE) required by company policy to put the sample system back in service – hard hat, safety shoes, goggles, flame retardant jacket and coveralls, and gloves. This PPE was, however, insufficient to protect against an acid spray injury

Tesoro operators informed the CSB that despite the fact that Tesoro Martinez required four items of PPE, the workers only wore the goggles and chemical gloves. The workers did not use the acid suit jacket required by the procedure because Tesoro stopped carrying this item in its PPE inventory
Failure to provide alkylation unit workers with necessary and functional personal protective equipment (PPE)

controls.name: Process Hazard Analysis (PHA) for Sampling System Hazards
controls.barrier_role: Identify and evaluate hazards associated with sulfuric acid sampling operations
controls.lod_basis: Foundational process safety management system to identify hazards and controls

supporting_text: In its 2009 process hazard analysis (PHA), Tesoro evaluated the hazards of sampling sulfuric acid. Tesoro's PHA documentation indicates that the PHA Team identified the sampling hazard to have a potential severity of a lost workday injury, which they anticipated to be likely to occur within 20 years. Tesoro's PHA risk matrix identifies this combination of severity and frequency to be 'low risk'

In its 2013 PHA, Tesoro did not specifically evaluate the hazards of the sulfuric acid sample stations. Instead, a human factors checklist was used to cover the sample systems. The checklist questions did not evaluate sampling system hazards

the report from Contra Costa County's inspection following the February 12, 2014 incident concluded that neither the 2009 nor the 2012 alkylation unit PHAs adequately evaluated the hazards associated with the acid sample stations

controls.name: Maintenance Planning and Documentation
controls.barrier_role: Ensure proper planning, documentation, and communication of maintenance activities
controls.lod_basis: Foundational process safety management system to ensure work is properly planned and executed

supporting_text: Due to maintenance program deficiencies including gaps in planning, documentation, and communication, Tesoro did not effectively repair or leak-test the tubing prior to placing it back in service

controls.name: Management of Change (MOC) Procedure
controls.barrier_role: Evaluate and control changes to equipment, processes, and procedures
controls.lod_basis: Process safety management system to ensure changes are properly evaluated and authorized

supporting_text: According to its incident investigation records, Tesoro installed this temporary pump without a Management of Change (MOC) and the pump failed because it was not suitable for sulfuric acid service

controls.name: Process Safety Culture and Management Commitment
controls.barrier_role: Establish organizational culture and management commitment to prioritize worker safety
controls.lod_basis: Foundational organizational control to ensure safety is prioritized across all operations

supporting_text: The safety culture at the Tesoro Martinez refinery created conditions conducive to the occurrence and recurrence of process safety incidents that caused worker injuries at the refinery over several years
Minimization of the seriousness of the February 12, 2014 process safety incident involving chemical burns to workers and a loss of primary containment

Despite the serious nature of the employee injuries and quantity of hazardous chemicals released from the loss of containment, Tesoro challenged the CSB's authority to investigate the incident and failed to preserve key evidence. Tesoro corporate officials characterized the incident as a minor personal safety event rather than a process safety incident

controls.name: Incident Investigation and Lessons Learned
controls.barrier_role: Investigate incidents and implement corrective actions to prevent recurrence
controls.lod_basis: Secondary control to learn from incidents and prevent recurrence

supporting_text: The CSB found that this second incident shared similar causation with a 1999 incident at the same refinery, then called the Avon refinery, owned by the Tosco Corporation, that resulted in four fatalities. Similarities between the two incidents suggest that the Tesoro Martinez refinery did not effectively continue to implement or communicate important safety lessons from the 1999 Tosco incident
Ineffective incorporation and communication of lessons learned from previous safety incidents

Tesoro tolerated numerous sulfuric acid worker injuries during the five-year period of 2010 to 2014. These incidents provide evidence of deficiencies in the refinery's organizational policies and practices governing safe work

controls.name: Safety Shower and Emergency Response
controls.barrier_role: Provide immediate decontamination and medical response for chemical exposure incidents
controls.lod_basis: Recovery control to mitigate consequences of chemical exposure

supporting_text: The operators immediately went to a nearby safety shower
Emergency responders transferred the workers by helicopter and ambulance to the UC Davis Regional Burn Center for evaluation and treatment of their sulfuric acid burn injuries

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
notes.additional_context: This incident is part of a pattern of sulfuric acid release incidents at the Tesoro Martinez refinery dating back to 2010. The CSB investigation identified systemic weaknesses in process safety culture, mechanical integrity, maintenance practices, and management commitment to safety. The refinery failed to implement lessons learned from a 1999 incident at the same facility that resulted in four fatalities. The investigation emphasizes the critical importance of strong safety culture and effective process safety management systems in preventing major accidents.
